license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.hgqb3vw
title: Chlorella Virus Plaque Assay
author: Dr. Steven Wilhelm

steps_raw.instruction: Melt MBBM soft agar and dispense in 3.0 mL aliquots and hold at 45ºC - 50ºC in a water bath
Concentrate Chlorella to 4.0*10 8 cells/mL at 5,000 rpm for 5 min at 4ºC and resuspend with fresh MBBM *Concentrate so that 0.3 mL can be used per plate

Dilute virus with 50 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.8 in 1/10 serial dilutions *Fresh lysate contains approximately 10 6 -10 7 PFU/mL (check the titer prior to plaque assay) *Dilute sample to have 20-200 plaque forming units (PFU) per plate

To each 3.0 mL soft agar aliquot, add 0.1 mL Chlorella virus and 0.3 mL Chlorella *Mix by rolling between palms and pour onto MBBM plate *Tilt the plate gently to allow the entire surface to be covered *Allow plate to solidify
Incubate at 25ºC in continuous light for 3-4 days or until plaques are visible to count *Use a sharpie to count plaques once there is a visible contrast
